title: gaultheria subcorymbosa
intro: Gaultheria subcorymbosa, also known as the New Caledonian Gaultheria, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is a small, evergreen shrub native to the tropical forests of New Caledonia, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves, stems, and roots. Gaultheria subcorymbosa contains compounds such as methyl salicylate and salicylic acid, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, providing health benefits in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
